reconciliation.scope: Release arc: v0.13.0 through evaluated_commit. The handshake SHAs (evaluated_commit / evaluated_tree / manifest_parent_commit) bind to N (the release-note commit), NOT to the assessed release-prep HEAD P. The release ceremony is forced into two commits because the migration note (templates/migrations/vX.Y.Z.md) must also land at HEAD: first note commit N (child of P), then this manifest-only commit M (child of N). At HEAD=M the handshake invariant requires HEAD^ == N == evaluated_commit == manifest_parent_commit AND HEAD^^{tree} == evaluated_tree == tree(N). Release mode is manifest-authority-only; the wrapper and CI recheck always read this committed manifest. records[] dispositions are operator-attested and unchanged by the releaser's SHA recompute. (v0.13.0 wording bug corrected: do NOT write evaluated_commit = P.)

records.summary: P1-LINEAGE-002 deferred coverage (F6/F7) — conditional on a future prune phase.
records.reason: Not part of the current release. Retained for audit with unsupported legacy trigger metadata (the source card's trigger grammar uses a '||' separator, a directory operand, and a non-predicate feature: term, which the strict release-mode predicate parser rejects). Re-evaluate only when a prune phase is pursued or renderstate/seam is touched for deletion logic. The canonical no+disclose+invalid disposition lets the release proceed while disclosing the conditional follow-up and its invalid metadata state.
